risks: CeoBrief component (344 lines) fetches markdown from an API endpoint — porting it into a game panel means either duplicating the fetch logic or extracting a shared hook. If the hook extraction is sloppy, both UIs break.
Sidebar simplification could break existing bookmark/deep-link URLs if nav items are removed rather than just hidden. Need to preserve all routes even if nav entries are consolidated.

Game panel theme (parchment/wood pixel-art via panelUtils.ts) may not render dense markdown content (CEO brief) well — could end up in a rabbit hole of styling a markdown renderer to look good in pixel-art frames.
over_engineering_flags: Temptation to build a fancy animated toggle with transitions between views — a simple icon button in the corner is sufficient.
Porting SchedulerCard fully into OpsPanel risks scope creep if the scheduler has interactive controls. Read-only summary is enough for game view.
challenges.recommendation: Simplify: One project. The toggle is trivial UI. CeoBrief and SchedulerCard ports should be read-only summaries using shared data hooks, not full component rewrites. Sidebar cleanup is just removing/renaming 2 nav items. No reason to split this.

projects.title: View Toggle + Game Panel Gap Fill + Sidebar Cleanup
projects.scope_summary: Add a small toggle button (likely in AppLayout or Sidebar) to switch between GamePage and DashboardPage. Port CeoBrief content into a game-themed panel (BriefPanel or MailboxPanel extension) using a shared data hook extracted from the existing CeoBrief component. Fold SchedulerCard summary info into OpsPanel as a read-only section. Simplify Sidebar by consolidating Office/Overview nav items into a single entry plus the toggle. Preserve all existing routes.
